core_mechanism: a scale-invariant reverse-Hölder (A-infinity-type) bound on the Radon–Nikodym density of one measure relative to a reference measure, self-improved by a Gehring higher-integrability bootstrap that pushes the density's integrability exponent strictly upward
brief_reason: The method's central estimate is a scale-invariant reverse-Hölder inequality for the density dω/dσ on boundary-centered balls, which a weighted Gehring argument then self-improves past exponent 2 to yield solvability across a range of exponents (the square estimates from the Pohozaev identity only serve to establish this reverse-Hölder bound).
